zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John le Strange de Felton於大約1289年出生在英國諾福爾可利其哈姆時，他的父親Sir Robert le Strange35歲，母親Eleanor Greystoke36歲。他和Sibyl至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於大約1346年在他的家鄉享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John le Strange de Felton于大约1289年出生在英国諾福爾可利其哈姆时，他的父亲Sir Robert le Strange35岁，母亲Eleanor Greystoke36岁。他和Sibyl至少有4个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于大约1346年在他的家乡享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John le Strange de Felton was born about 1289, in Litcham, Norfolk, England, his father, Sir Robert le Strange, was 35 and his mother, Eleanor Greystoke, was 36. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Sibyl. He died about 1346, in his hometown, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John le Strange de Felton est né environ 1289, à Litcham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert le Strange, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Greystoke, avait 36 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Sibyl. Il est décédé environ 1346, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John le Strange de Felton um 1289 in Litcham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Robert le Strange 35 und seine Mutter Eleanor Greystoke 36. Er hatte mit Sibyl mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1346 im Alter von 59 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John le Strange de Felton è nato il circa 1289 nel Litcham, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Sir Robert le Strange, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor Greystoke, aveva 36 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Sibyl. Morì circa 1346, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John le Strange de Feltonが約1289年にイギリスノーフォルクリトチャムで生まれた時，父Sir Robert le Strangeは35歳，母Eleanor Greystokeは36歳であった。彼はSibylとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は約1346年に故郷で59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John le Strange de Felton(이)가 대략 1289년 영국 노르폴크 리트챔에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert le Strange(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor Greystoke(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Sibyl(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1346년 그의 고향에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John le Strange de Felton nasceu aproximadamente 1289, em Litcham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert le Strange, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Greystoke, tinha 36 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas com Sibyl. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1346, em sua cidade natal, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John le Strange de Felton родился около 1289 г. в местности личам, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert le Strange было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Greystoke, 36. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Sibyl. Он умер около 1346 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 59 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas con Sibyl. Murió aproximadamente 1346, en su pueblo, a la edad de 59 años.
